findings.evidence: `rg -n "theorem (id|of_opens|prodMap|sumInl|sumInr)_(coe|apply)|ofIsSmoothEmbedding_coe" code/TauCeti/Geometry/Manifold/SmoothEmbedding.lean` shows `ofIsSmoothEmbedding_coe` at line 115 but only `*_apply` lemmas for the other constructors at lines 136, 149, 163, 178, and 192.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` coercion lemmas such as `id_coe`, `of_opens_coe`, `prodMap_coe`, `sumInl_coe`, and `sumInr_coe`, proved from the existing apply lemmas without exposing the constructor bodies.
findings.issue: The public constructors `id`, `of_opens`, `prodMap`, `sumInl`, and `sumInr` only have pointwise `*_apply` simp lemmas, unlike `ofIsSmoothEmbedding`, which also has a coercion lemma; goals about the underlying function cannot normalize directly by `simp` without manual `funext`.

summary: The bundle hides constructor bodies and has the basic predicate accessors, but the constructor API is incomplete at the function-coercion level. Downstream users should not need to unfold or manually ext every time they use these constructors as maps.
